M-041.NAME: verified-email-registration
M-041.path: backend/app/users backend/app/email backend/tests/test_trial_activation.py scripts/phase36-resend-deploy-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-27.xml docs/plans/Phase-28.xml docs/plans/Phase-36.xml docs/plans/Phase-45.xml
M-041.owns: Pending email registration, email ownership verification, disposable-domain guard, Phase-28 post-verification onboarding orchestration, and Phase-45 pending trial handoff.
M-041.purpose: Build the verified-registration foundation so KrotPN can create user access, pending trial access, VPN config, referral effects, and MTProto proxy only after email ownership is proven.

exports.fn-request-registration: Validate email/password/referral input and send a one-time verification email without creating active access.
exports.fn-verify-registration: Consume verification token once for low-level token lifecycle checks.
exports.fn-activate-registration: Consume verification token once, create verified user, and hand off onboarding side effects after ownership proof, including pending VPN trial access.
exports.fn-email-risk-check: Evaluate syntax, domain, MX/DNS, disposable-domain, and configured deny/allow policies.
exports.fn-registration-status: Return safe pending, verified, expired, or rejected registration state for the frontend.

registration-policy.policy-1: Phase-27 kept active /register behavior unchanged while pending-registration primitives were verified.
registration-policy.policy-2: Phase-28 changes plain /register so active user access is not created before email verification.
registration-policy.policy-3: Trial access is a four-day pending trial after verified activation; the countdown starts only after the first successful VPN handshake.
registration-policy.policy-4: Telegram auth remains a separate verified identity path unless explicitly merged later.

implementation-state.state-1: Phase-27 implemented PendingEmailRegistration, HMAC token hashing, TTL/replay-safe token consumption, email normalization, allow/deny/disposable-domain policy, and optional DNS guard.
implementation-state.state-2: Phase-28 implements /register pending response, /verify-email activation, post-verification trial/VPN/referral orchestration, replay-safe activation, and frontend onboarding states.
implementation-state.state-3: MTProto assignment remains deferred to Phase-29+ and is not issued in Phase-28.
implementation-state.state-4: Phase-34 local integration verifies verified activation can expose one owner MTProto assignment while pending/unverified registration creates none.
implementation-state.state-5: Phase-36 wires production Resend deploy settings so pending registration sends verification emails on clean VPS deployments after operator Resend domain verification.
implementation-state.state-6: Phase-45 changes verified activation to create pending trial access only; countdown activation is owned by M-063/M-004 after first VPN handshake.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not issue trial, VPN device, referral bonus, or MTProto proxy for unverified email.
constraints.constraint-2: Verification token consumption must be one-time, TTL-bound, and replay-safe.
constraints.constraint-3: Temp-mail blocking is defense-in-depth; email ownership proof remains the decisive gate.
constraints.constraint-4: Production /register must fail with an operator-actionable provider configuration error only when Resend env values are missing or rejected, not because the provider is left disabled by deploy defaults.

phase-36-plan.step-1: Verify /register calls M-040 with EMAIL_PROVIDER=resend and does not produce provider_disabled after deploy wiring is present.
phase-36-plan.step-2: Keep all pre-verification side-effect guards unchanged while enabling live email delivery.
phase-36-plan.step-3: Add live handoff notes for controlled-mailbox registration smoke after Resend domain verification completes.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-17: Phase-36 local verification keeps registration tests on fake providers and leaves controlled-mailbox Resend smoke operator-only.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-19: Operator clean-deploy registration via email succeeded with Resend configured while preserving pending-before-verification behavior.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-01: Phase-45 backend suite passed and proves verified-email activation creates pending four-day trial access without starting the countdown before first VPN handshake.

M-041.hotspot: H-027: Trial, VPN provisioning, referral effects, and MTProto issuance must not happen before email ownership is verified
